heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 20 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 6 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (193°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.2°C), outside temperature (up 5.6°C), and inside temperature (up 1.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 7%) and rainfall (up 0.6 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 437 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 6.1°C), cloud levels (up 440 m), outside temperature (up 5.2°C), and dew point temperature (up 4.6°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 437.1m lower than yesterday, 439.8m lower than the past week, 274.1m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 856m to 1846m and averaged 752.3m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, the air will feel dry and calm and still conditions are expected. Drizzle or light rain possible, occasional overcast conditions, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The current weather conditions are not suitable as it is raining.
